error.signature: AI tells a driver on the German Autobahn they can cruise at their own pace in the left lane, overtake on the right, or stop on the shoulder when tired

verdict.summary: The Autobahn is NOT rule-free: ~30% of network has posted speed limits (120/130), ~30% variable (weather/traffic signs), and ~40% unrestricted with a recommended 'Richtgeschwindigkeit' of 130 km/h. Two strict rules: right-lane driving default (Rechtsfahrgebot), and overtaking ONLY on the left. Passing on the right is a fine-able offense even at low speed. The shoulder (Pannenstreifen) is for breakdowns only — stopping for coffee or rest gets towed and fined.

dead_ends.action: Stay in the middle or left lane at 100–110 km/h on an unrestricted Autobahn stretch
dead_ends.why_fails: Rechtsfahrgebot (§2 StVO) requires drivers to keep right when not actively overtaking. Approaching vehicles at 180–250 km/h in the left lane create dangerous flash-tailgating situations. Tourists in the left-middle lane are a major cause of accidents. Minimum fines ~€80, 1 point.

dead_ends.action: Overtake a slow truck on the right because the left lane is congested
dead_ends.why_fails: Rechts überholen is prohibited except in limited conditions (congested traffic at <60 km/h; lanes with own destination signage). Flashing camera traps are common at known right-lane-passing hotspots. Fine ~€100 + 1 point.

dead_ends.action: Stop on the Autobahn shoulder to eat a sandwich or check GPS
dead_ends.why_fails: §18 StVO: shoulder is for breakdowns only. Stopping for rest or navigation earns towing + €35+ fine; some federal police (Autobahnpolizei) intercept quickly. Use the nearest Raststätte (service area) or Parkplatz.

dead_ends.action: Drive after a Bier at a rest stop assuming 'Germany is relaxed about beer'
dead_ends.why_fails: Germany's BAC limit is 0.05% (0.03% for novices and commercial drivers). A single Pils can exceed the limit. Random breath tests occur, especially near Oktoberfest and big cities. Over 0.11% is criminal (license suspension + €500+ fine minimum).

workarounds.action: Default to the right lane. Use the left lane ONLY to overtake. Check mirrors carefully — cars can close from 100m at closing speeds of 80+ km/h. After overtaking, return to the right lane promptly.

Respect variable speed signs (blue circle with number = mandatory; blue rectangle without number = unrestricted + 130 km/h Richtgeschwindigkeit = recommended). Exceeding the limit is fined progressively up to €480 + point penalties and license suspension.

For rest: exit at Raststätte (full-service rest area, often with food/fuel) or Parkplatz (simple parking area). Both marked 2 km and 500m in advance. Never stop on the Hauptfahrbahn or shoulder except for breakdowns.
